steps.text: Find the proportion of supporters in the sample: $$\frac{10}{25} = 0.40$$
Apply this proportion to the full population of 60 board members: $$0.40 \times 60 = 24$$

• A — 10 is just the raw sample count, not scaled to the full population. Eliminated.
  • B — 14 does not correspond to any proportional calculation from these numbers. Eliminated.
  • C — 24 equals $0.40 \times 60$, the sample proportion applied to the full board. That confirms C is correct.
  • D — 40 is the sample support percent, not the estimated number of board members who support the proposal. Eliminated.
